raw_text:
CloudVeil Systems // Junior Site Reliability Engineer

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly) | Full-time | $85,000 - $105,000 per year

Making infrastructure invisible so your business can shine

>> ABOUT

CloudVeil Systems is a fast-growing infrastructure automation company founded in 2019,
focused on helping mid-market to enterprise organizations build resilient, self-healing

cloud platforms. We specialize in observability, incident response automation, and
reliability engineering tooling for containerized environments. Our team of 120+

engineers is distributed across North America and Europe.

>> SUMMARY

Join CloudVeil Systems as a Junior Site Reliability Engineer and help build the future of

cloud reliability. In this role, you'll work alongside experienced SREs to design,
implement, and maintain the infrastructure and monitoring systems that keep our platform

and customer deployments running smoothly. You'll gain hands-on experience with
Kubernetes, observability tools, and incident response in a supportive, learning-focused

environment.

>> RESPONSIBILITIES

- Assist in deploying and managing containerized applications on Kubernetes clusters,

including troubleshooting pod scheduling and resource allocation issues
- Help implement and maintain Prometheus metrics collection and alerting rules,

learning best practices for SLO/SLI definition and monitoring
- Support on-call rotation by responding to alerts, escalating incidents

appropriately, and participating in blameless post-mortems
- Write Python scripts to automate routine operational tasks such as log parsing,

capacity planning data collection, and deployment validation
- Contribute to runbooks and documentation for common operational procedures and

incident response workflows
- Monitor application performance using Grafana dashboards and distributed tracing

tools (Jaeger/Tempo), identifying bottlenecks and anomalies
- Collaborate with platform and backend teams to integrate their services into our

observability stack and improve their reliability metrics
- Participate in incident response drills and learn to use our incident management

tools and communication protocols

>> MUST HAVE

- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent professional

experience (0-2 years as SRE, DevOps, or backend engineer)
- Foundational knowledge of Linux system administration and shell scripting (Bash)

- Experience with containerization concepts and basic Docker usage
- Familiarity with at least one programming language (Python, Go, or similar) for

writing automation scripts
- Understanding of networking basics (DNS, TCP/IP, load balancing) and how they relate

to cloud infrastructure

>> NICE TO HAVE

 - Prior exposure to Kubernetes (minikube, GKE, or self-managed clusters)

- Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools such as Terraform or Helm
- Knowledge of database concepts and exposure to managing stateful systems on

Kubernetes
- Familiarity with Git workflows and CI/CD pipelines

>> STACK

Kubernetes / Prometheus / Grafana / Jaeger (Distributed Tracing) / Python / Go / Terraform /

Helm / ELK Stack / Loki / PagerDuty

>> BENEFITS

- Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% employer coverage
- 401(k) matching program (up to 4% of salary)

- Unlimited paid time off with minimum 15 days annually
- Home office equipment stipend ($1,500)

- Professional development budget ($2,000/year) for conferences, courses, and
certifications

- Mentorship program pairing junior engineers with senior SREs
- Team offsites and company retreats twice yearly

- Flexible work hours and remote-first culture

>> APPLY

Submit your resume and a brief cover letter explaining your interest in SRE work to

careers@cloudveil.io. Include links to any GitHub projects or deployments you've worked
on. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an initial phone screening followed by a

technical assessment and team interviews.
